objective: Run a first governed native GitHub auto-merge pilot without weakening protected branch checks, rulesets, reviews, CODEOWNERS, or rollback controls.
scope_boundary: Capture pre-change repository settings, repo rulesets, org rulesets, and branch-protection evidence.
Enable only the native GitHub repository auto-merge setting for hldpro-governance.
Open a pilot PR with explicit merge-when-green opt-in and enable native PR auto-merge after required checks are queued.
Record dry-run evaluator evidence and rollback/fix-forward notes.
out_of_scope: Weakening rulesets, required checks, review requirements, CODEOWNERS, Local CI Gate, or workflow definitions.
Expanding auto-merge to other repositories before pilot evidence is recorded.
Using admin bypass or manual merge as pilot success evidence.
research_summary: Issue #386 established the accepted policy: automerge is allowed only as a native GitHub accelerator after branch protection and required checks have already accepted a PR. Pre-change inspection for hldpro-governance shows repository auto-merge disabled, classic branch protection absent, repo ruleset 15241047 requiring Local CI Gate on main, and org ruleset 14715976 protecting main branches. Therefore the pilot can change only allow_auto_merge and must rely on the existing rulesets and PR checks.

sprints.name: Native auto-merge pilot
sprints.goal: Prove a single hldpro-governance PR can be merged by native GitHub auto-merge after required checks pass.
tasks: Capture pre-change repository and protection snapshots.
Commit pilot PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, and validation evidence.
Create or reuse merge-when-green opt-in label.
Open the pilot PR and add the opt-in label.
Run local gates and policy evaluator fixtures.
Enable PR-level native auto-merge and observe GitHub merge result.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-change snapshots show the rollback baseline.
No ruleset, required check, review, CODEOWNER, or workflow weakening occurs.
Local CI Gate and GitHub required checks pass.
Pilot PR is merged by native GitHub auto-merge, or rollback evidence is recorded.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify live repo settings and protection state before enabling native auto-merge.
specialist_reviews.summary: Pilot may proceed by changing only allow_auto_merge and relying on existing protected-branch/ruleset checks.

alternate_model_review.summary: The accepted #386 policy authorizes a native GitHub pilot as separate issue-backed work with rollback snapshots.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #391 may capture GitHub settings evidence, enable repository native auto-merge, open one pilot PR, enable PR-level native auto-merge, and record rollback evidence. Rulesets, required checks, review rules, CODEOWNERS, and workflows are out of scope.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate local gates, enable repository allow_auto_merge, open the pilot PR, and enable native PR auto-merge after checks are queued.

material_deviation_rules: If any step requires weakening required checks, rulesets, reviews, or CODEOWNERS, stop and rollback.
If native auto-merge cannot be enabled, record the blocker and do not merge manually as pilot evidence.
If checks fail, do not bypass; fix forward or disable repository auto-merge and remove the opt-in label.
